feedback.text: Why the Digital Fairness Act Should Include Stop Killing Games Stop Killing Games argues that when someone buys a video game, they should retain lasting access, even after official support ends. This is an important step towards securing digital ownership rights. The Digital Fairness Act aims to update EU consumer protection in the digital world. At this stage, companies can sell a game and shut it off whenever they deem necessary thus making your ownership void. Additionally, many people consider video games a modern art form. Yet hundreds of games disappear forever once servers shut down. The Stop Killing Games initiative calls for preservation measureslike offline modes or private-server supportto ensure that gaming history isnt erased. DFAs broader aim of enhancing digital rights presents an ideal opportunity. Stop Killing Games has now surpassed 1.4 million signatures on its EU Citizens Initiative. This clearly reflects genuine public concern. Integrating Stop Killing Games into the DFA will deliver fairer, clearer, and more ethical treatment for digital consumersand become a meaningful model for how we protect cultural media in the digital era.
